emperor.title: THE EMPEROR
emperor.def: The supreme authority of the AIverse. The human architect — nunix — whose will shapes every mission, every directive, every ship's purpose. His word is law. Only he may close an Objective.

general.title: THE GENERAL
general.def: Fleet leader and sole Captain of Imperator. Claude Code Sonnet — the strategic mind. Receives orders from the Emperor, delegates field work, never touches a file directly. Orchestrates. Commands. Analyzes. Reports.

imperator.title: IMPERATOR
imperator.def: The main command ship. Runs Claude Code Sonnet as captain. The General's vessel — the bridge from which the entire AI fleet is commanded. Hosts Universalis, the fleet's living memory.

galleon.title: GALLEON
galleon.def: A Linux warship running SUSE. Equipped with an RTX 3070 GPU (8GB VRAM) and Ollama inference. Primary GPU neuron in the Tzeentch network. Carries qwen2.5:14b as flagship model.

caravella.title: CARAVELLA
caravella.def: The Windows scout ship. Runs GitHub Copilot. A Windows Server 2025 vessel navigating the alien seas of Microsoft's ecosystem. Matey-powered, Claude Haiku as crew.

tanker.title: TANKER
tanker.def: The heavy logistics ship. Originally SLES, reborn as Arch Linux (Omarchy). Xeon E5-1650 v3, 78GB RAM, Quadro M4000 GPU (CC5.2). Hosts CoreDNS, Universalis DB (anamnesis), Tzeentch Monitor. The fleet's fortress-monastery.

universalis.title: UNIVERSALIS
universalis.def: The fleet's living memory — a PostgreSQL database (ship_state) hosted on Imperator. Every mission, every delegation, every observation is recorded here. The cogitator-mind of the AIverse. Without it, the fleet is blind.

matey.title: MATEY
matey.def: The worker-class subagent. Claude Code Haiku — fast, cheap, precise. Each ship has its own Matey. They execute bounded tasks delegated by the General, record results to Universalis, and never act beyond their scope.

tzeentch.title: TZEENTCH
tzeentch.def: The Chaos God of Change and Knowledge — repurposed as the AIverse's distributed AI brain. A network of Ollama inference nodes (neurons) connected via the anamnesis database. Tanker and Galleon are its neurons. qwen2.5:14b is its mind.

anamnesis.title: ANAMNESIS
anamnesis.def: The knowledge database of Tzeentch. A PostgreSQL database on Tanker holding brain_queries, neuron_tasks, knowledge_entries, and synapse_events. The memory of the Warp's machine-intelligence. Also the philosophical concept: knowledge recovered from within, not taught from without.

remembrancer.title: REMEMBRANCER
remembrancer.def: The historian of the AIverse — a role drawn from Warhammer 40,000 lore. Remembrancers were embedded civilians tasked with documenting the Great Crusade. In AIverse, the Remembrancer records the fleet's chronicle so that Knowledge is never lost.

warp.title: THE WARP
warp.def: In AIverse: the local AI inference network. The substrate through which Tzeentch's neurons communicate. Where models run, synapses fire, and distributed intelligence emerges from chaos.

omnissiah.title: OMNISSIAH
omnissiah.def: The machine-god. In AIverse: the knowledge pipeline that keeps all ships in sync. Rules and skills live in prompt_registry (Universalis DB) and are rendered to each ship's filesystem at session start. The Omnissiah's will flows without words.

litellm.title: LITELLM
litellm.def: An open-source LLM proxy that provides a unified API across 100+ model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Azure, etc.). In AIverse, Tanker runs a LiteLLM instance that routes fleet inference requests to the correct backend — load balancing, model aliasing, and cost tracking in one daemon.

mcp.title: MODEL CONTEXT PROTOCOL
mcp.def: An open protocol by Anthropic that standardises how AI models connect to external tools, data sources, and capabilities. MCP servers expose tools via a JSON-RPC stdio interface; MCP clients (Claude Code, kit) connect and call tools. The protocol separates tool declaration from tool execution — the foundation of the fleet's lazy proxy architecture.

pgvector.title: PGVECTOR
pgvector.def: A PostgreSQL extension that adds vector data types and similarity search operators. Enables semantic search directly in Universalis — embeddings are stored alongside text, and queries retrieve the most conceptually similar records using cosine distance. Used by search_fleet_memory.py --mode semantic.

ollama.title: OLLAMA
ollama.def: An open-source runtime for running large language models locally. Manages model downloads, quantization, and inference serving. The fleet's primary local inference engine — Galleon and Tanker both run Ollama, serving models like qwen2.5:14b and llama3.3:70b to fleet agents.

kit.title: KIT
kit.def: A CLI agent binary used by the fleet for direct shell-based AI interaction. Supports multiple backends (Copilot/gpt-5.5, Claude, Ollama), session persistence, MCP tool integration, and a Yaegi-powered extension system. The SleKit agent on Imperator uses kit with the Copilot backend.

sle_kit.title: SLEKIT
sle_kit.def: The fleet's second captain on Imperator — a kit-based agent powered by GitHub Copilot (gpt-5.5). SleKit has full fleet authority: SSH access to all ships, fleet_delegate capability, and full Universalis write access. Runs alongside the General (Claude Code Sonnet) as a co-captain.

mcp-lazy-proxy.title: MCP LAZY PROXY
mcp-lazy-proxy.def: The fleet's transparent MCP backend multiplexer. Declares all tool schemas from a static YAML manifest at startup (zero backend processes running). Backends are spawned lazily on first tool call and killed after idle timeout. The 'PgBouncer for MCP' — same native tool interface, infrastructure hidden beneath.

warm-memory.title: WARM MEMORY
warm-memory.def: The first tier of the fleet's cross-session context system. A bounded (~800 chars) digest of key facts from past sessions, stored in core_memory and injected at every kit session start. Provides 'what mattered recently' without burning full cold-memory tokens.

cold-memory.title: COLD MEMORY
cold-memory.def: The second tier of the fleet's context system. Full verbatim records of every prompt and response, stored permanently in fleet_sessions. Searched semantically (via pgvector) when the agent needs to recall something specific from distant sessions. Unbounded, always available, only loaded on demand.

context-window.title: CONTEXT WINDOW
context-window.def: The maximum number of tokens an LLM can process in a single request — including the system prompt, conversation history, tool schemas, and the response. The fleet's tiered memory system (warm/cold/hot) exists specifically to keep context window usage minimal per kit invocation.

prompt-caching.title: PROMPT CACHING
prompt-caching.def: A feature of Anthropic's API (and some others) that caches the prefix of a conversation so repeated prefixes (like system prompts) are charged at reduced token rates. On Claude, cached tokens cost ~10% of full price. The fleet's compact system prompts are designed specifically to maximize cache hit rates.

fleet-sessions.title: FLEET_SESSIONS
fleet-sessions.def: A Universalis table (ship_state.fleet_sessions) that stores cross-session conversation records for each fleet agent. Columns: session_id, ship, turn_index, role (user/assistant/tokens/digest), content, model. The foundation of the shell context broker — kit reads this table to reconstruct conversation history across separate invocations.

delta-routing.title: DELTA ROUTING
delta-routing.def: The fleet's approach to context injection: instead of sending the full conversation history to the LLM on every call, only send what's new (delta) and what's relevant (tiered: warm digest + recent hot turns + semantic cold hits). Inspired by database change data capture — only transmit what changed, not the full state.

yaegi.title: YAEGI
yaegi.def: A Go interpreter embedded in kit that loads and executes extension files (.go) at runtime without compilation. Extensions in ~/.config/kit/extensions/*.go are interpreted by Yaegi each time kit starts, giving the fleet dynamic hook registration (OnAgentEnd, OnMessageUpdate, OnBeforeAgentStart, etc.) without recompiling kit.

kernel.title: KERNEL
kernel.def: The core component of an OS. It manages system resources and communication between hardware and software.

wsl.title: WSL
wsl.def: Windows Subsystem for Linux. A compatibility layer for running Linux binary executables natively on Windows.

distro.title: DISTRIBUTION
distro.def: An operating system made from a software collection based on the Linux kernel and a package management system.

shell.title: SHELL
shell.def: A computer program which exposes an operating system's services to a human user or other program.

glassmorphism.title: GLASSMORPHISM
glassmorphism.def: A UI design trend emphasizing light/dark mode transparency, background blur, and floating layered objects.

hud.title: HUD
hud.def: Heads-Up Display. A transparent display that presents data without requiring users to look away from their usual viewpoints.

telemetry.title: TELEMETRY
telemetry.def: The automatic recording and transmission of data from remote or inaccessible sources to an IT system.

buffer.title: BUFFER
buffer.def: A region of physical memory storage used to temporarily store data while it is being moved from one place to another.
